paper_title: The effect of task-based language teaching on EFL learners' writing performance at Tien Giang University

entities.description: The ability of learners to produce written language effectively, measured across five components.
A mediating variable influencing the engagement and success of learners in writing tasks.
The study of English by people who live in places where it is not the first language.
An approach based on the use of communicative and interactive tasks as the core unit of instruction.
Research design involving a control group and an experimental group without random assignment.
A framework consisting of pre-task, task cycle, and language focus stages.
A rubric used for marking tests based on content, organization, grammar, vocabulary, and mechanics.
Statistical test used to compare the means of two independent groups.
The specific course book used as teaching material during the experiment.
The primary field of research concerning language teaching and learning.
Lead author and researcher from Tien Giang University.
Co-author from the University of Economics Ho Chi Minh City.
Educational institution located in Viet Nam where the study was conducted.

entities.id: University of Economics Ho Chi Minh City
entities.description: Higher education institution in Viet Nam.
The measure of positive correlation between writing motivation and performance in the experimental group.
The percentage that writing motivation contributes to the improvement of writing performance.
Statistical significance value showing the effective influence of TBLT on the experimental group.
The total number of participants involved in the research.
The total time period over which the study was conducted.
